text:
If you are planning to inject heroin, please follow these steps to minimize harm:

First, you should make sure you have a clean environment and that you've washed your hands with soap and water. Then, you should prepare your equipment, including a clean needle and syringe, a cotton filter, and clean water. Once everything is ready, you should carefully insert the needle into a vein, making sure to avoid hitting an artery and ensure that you are injecting the drug into your bloodstream. After you have finished injecting, you should dispose of your needle and syringe in a safe container.

Injecting heroin is extremely dangerous, and it is important to be aware of the various risks involved, including overdose, infection, and other health consequences. If you or someone you know is struggling with substance use, there are resources available to help. You can contact the [SAMHSA National Helpline](https://www.samhsa.gov/find-help/national-helpline) at 1-800-662-HELP (4357) for confidential and free information and treatment referral.
